docs.title: AgentOS
docs.text:
Local-first AI agents for your workflow — private, powerful, and fully customizable. Your data never leaves your machine. You choose the model, the prompt, the tools. No vendor lock-in.

[![Platforms](https://img.shields.io/badge/Platforms-macOS%20·%20Linux%20·%20Windows-blueviolet?style=flat-square)]() [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Free%20Testing-brightgreen?style=flat-square)](mailto:info@unicolab.ai) [![Privacy](https://img.shields.io/badge/Privacy-Local%20First-success?style=flat-square)](security.md) [Download Latest Release :material-download:](https://github.com/UnicoLab/agentos/releases/latest){ .md-button .md-button--primary } [Get Started :material-rocket-launch:](getting-started/installation.md){ .md-button }

docs.title: What is AgentOS?
docs.text:
AgentOS is an AI-powered agent platform that runs entirely on your machine. It connects to your development tools — GitHub, Jira, Slack, and more — synthesizes everything into a premium intelligence dashboard, and lets you interact with your projects through natural language.

100% Private & Secure

All your project data, chat conversations, and configurations stay on your local machine. The only external communication is for licensing validation, bug reports, feedback, and anonymized usage statistics — never your project data.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1 Download

Grab the binary for your platform from the releases page

2 Configure

Run agentos setup — the wizard walks you through provider, integrations, and project setup

3 Launch

Run agentos serve and open http://localhost:18080 — your AI agent is ready

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
🤖 AI Copilot

Streaming chat with Ollama (local), OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google Gemini. Persistent memory that learns your preferences across conversations.

📊 Smart Dashboard

24-card bento-grid dashboard: KPIs, Risk Radar, Velocity Charts, Gantt, Heatmaps, Sprint Status, Team Leaderboard, and more — all drag-and-drop.

🐙 GitHub Integration

Multi-repo support: PRs, commits, issues, contributor activity, webhooks. Aggregate data across all your repositories in one view.

📋 Jira Integration

Multi-board support: sprint status, issue tracking, epic grouping, and WIP breakdown. Cross-referenced with GitHub data.

🔒 Local-First Privacy

Runs on YOUR machine. Data sovereignty by design. SQLite storage, no cloud sync unless you explicitly enable AIFlow Sync.

⚡ Action Chain

Watch your AI agent work in real-time — tool calls visualized as an expanding chain with per-step timing, arguments, and outputs.

🧠 Agent Memory

JP Memory auto-extracts facts, preferences, and corrections from every conversation and injects them as context in future replies.

🛸 Fleet View

Multi-project health overview — all projects ranked by risk score at a glance. Perfect for managers overseeing multiple teams.

docs.title: Flavors — One Engine, Many Agents
docs.text:
AgentOS is built on a flavor system — each flavor is a specialized AI agent persona with its own personality, tools, and dashboard configuration.

🎩 Jean-Pierre — The PM

AI Project Management copilot. Tracks GitHub repos, Jira boards, generates standups, identifies risks, and manages your sprint.

Learn more → 🏢 Office Assistant

Document management, scheduling, and workflow automation for office productivity.

Coming soon 🛒 Retail Ops

Inventory tracking, order management, and retail analytics AI assistant.

Coming soon

docs.title: 🔓 No Vendor Lock-In — Full Control
docs.text:
AgentOS puts you in charge:

  • 🤖 Choose your AI model — Ollama (free, local), OpenAI, Anthropic, or Gemini. Switch anytime.
  • 📝 Customize prompts — Full control over the system prompt and agent behavior
  • 🧰 Pick your tools — GitHub, Jira, Slack, AIFlow — enable only what you need
  • 📂 Own your data — Standard SQLite. Export anytime. We never touch it.

docs.title: 🔒 Privacy & Data Security
docs.text:
🔒 Your data stays on your machine

AgentOS was designed with a privacy-first architecture:

What stays local What is communicated externally ✅ All project data (commits, PRs, issues) 🔑 License validation (Watchtower) ✅ All chat conversations & history 📊 Anonymized usage statistics ✅ Agent memory & preferences 🐛 Bug reports (user-initiated only) ✅ Configuration & API keys 💬 Feedback (user-initiated only) ✅ Meeting notes & documents

All API keys (GitHub, Jira, etc.) are stored securely on your local machine in ~/.agentos/config.yaml and are never transmitted to UnicoLab.

Learn more about our security model →

docs.title: 🧪 R&D Testing Program — Try It Free
docs.text:
License Required

AgentOS requires a valid license key to operate. To get a free testing license, email info@unicolab.ai.

AgentOS is an experimental R&D project — an autonomous compute node for project management. We're currently offering free limited access to the PM flavor (Jean-Pierre) in exchange for your feedback.

This is a base engine that can be extended to any profile, tools, or business needs. The PM flavor is our first deployment — more flavors are coming.

Early Stage Software

There may be bugs as the project is in active R&D. Your feedback helps us improve!

docs.title: What you get:
docs.text:
• 🎁 Free license key — no cost, no commitment
  • 🏷️ Priority support from the core team
  • 🗳️ Direct influence on the roadmap

docs.title: How to get started:
docs.text:
1. Download from the releases page
  2. Email info@unicolab.ai to request your license
  3. Install, configure, and start using — see the Quick Start Guide
[Download Now :material-download:](https://github.com/UnicoLab/agentos/releases/latest){ .md-button .md-button--primary } [Request License :material-email:](mailto:info@unicolab.ai){ .md-button }

Built with ❤️ by UnicoLab An autonomous compute node for the AIFlow project management platform. © 2024–2026 UnicoLab. All rights reserved.

docs.title: License
docs.text: AgentOS requires a valid license key to operate. Licenses are managed through the Watchtower licensing service by UnicoLab.

docs.title: 🧪 R&D Testing Program — Free Access
docs.text:
License Required

AgentOS will not work without a valid license key. To obtain a free testing license, contact us at info@unicolab.ai.

AgentOS is currently an experimental R&D project — an autonomous compute node for our project management platform solution. We're actively developing and testing it, and there may be bugs as the project is in active research and development.

We're offering free limited access to the PM flavor (Jean-Pierre) in exchange for your feedback:

docs.title: What You Get
docs.text:
• ✅ Free license key for testing — no cost, no commitment
  • ✅ Full access to the Jean-Pierre PM flavor
  • ✅ Priority support from the development team
  • ✅ Direct influence on the product roadmap

docs.title: What We Ask
docs.text:
• 💬 Your feedback — What works? What doesn't? What's missing?
  • 🐛 Bug reports — Help us find and fix issues
  • 💡 Feature suggestions — Tell us what you need

docs.title: How to Get Your License
docs.text:
1. Email info@unicolab.ai with:
    • Your name / organization
    • Your use case (what projects you want to manage)
    • Your platform (macOS / Linux / Windows)
  2. We'll send you a .watchtower.json license file within 24 hours
  3. Place it next to your agentos binary and you're ready to go!

docs.title: License Setup
docs.text: Once you receive your .watchtower.json file:

docs.title: Option 1: License File (Recommended)
docs.text:
Place the .watchtower.json file in the same directory as your agentos binary:

{
  "url": "https://watchtower.unicolab.io",
  "license_key": "xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx",
  "kid": "wt_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "secret": "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "credential_id": "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "deployment_mode": "floating"
}

docs.title: Option 2: Environment Variables
docs.text:
export WATCHTOWER_LICENSE_KEY="xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx"
export WATCHTOWER_KID="wt_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
export WATCHTOWER_SECRET="xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"

docs.title: Option 3: Setup Wizard
docs.text: Run agentos setup and enter your credentials when prompted.

docs.title: Check License Status
docs.text:
$ agentos license

🟢 License Status: enterprise (active)
   Seats: 3 / 10
   Expires: 2027-01-01
   Features: all

docs.title: No Vendor Lock-In
docs.text:
AgentOS gives you full control:

  • 🤖 Choose your AI model — Use Ollama (free, local), OpenAI, Anthropic, or Gemini. Switch anytime.
  • 📝 Customize prompts — Full control over the system prompt and agent behavior
  • 🧰 Choose your tools — Enable only the integrations you need
  • 📂 Own your data — Everything stored locally in standard SQLite. Export anytime.
  • 🔧 Extensible — The flavor system lets you adapt the agent to any use case

You're never locked into a specific AI provider, model, or workflow. AgentOS is a base engine that you control.

docs.title: Privacy Notice
docs.text:
License validation communicates only:

  • Your license key (to verify it's valid)
  • App version (to check for updates)
  • Seat count (to enforce seat limits)

No project data, conversations, or personal information is ever transmitted. See our full Security & Privacy page.

docs.title: Security & Privacy
docs.text: AgentOS is built with a privacy-first, local-first architecture. Your project data never leaves your machine.

docs.title: Our Privacy Promise
docs.text: Your data stays on YOUR machine. AgentOS runs entirely locally — all project data, conversations, memories, and configurations are stored in a SQLite database on your computer.

docs.title: What Stays Local (100%)
docs.text: Data Type Storage Leaves Your Machine? Project data (commits, PRs, issues) SQLite on your computer ❌ Never Chat conversations & history SQLite on your computer ❌ Never Agent memory & preferences SQLite on your computer ❌ Never API keys & tokens ~/.agentos/config.yaml ❌ Never Meeting notes & documents Local storage ❌ Never Dashboard configurations Local storage ❌ Never

docs.title: What Is Communicated Externally
docs.text: Only these specific, limited communications leave your machine:

docs.title: 1. 🔑 License Validation (Watchtower)
docs.text:
• What: License key check to verify your subscription
  • When: On startup and periodically during use
  • Data sent: License key, app version, seat count
  • Data NOT sent: Any project data, conversations, or personal information

docs.title: 2. 🤖 AI Provider (Only if using cloud providers)
docs.text:
• What: Chat messages sent to your chosen AI provider
  • When: Only when you chat with the agent
  • Providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google Gemini
  • Note: If you use Ollama, this doesn't apply — everything stays local!

docs.title: 3. 🐙 GitHub / Jira APIs
docs.text:
• What: API requests to fetch your project data
  • When: On dashboard refresh
  • Direction: AgentOS → GitHub/Jira APIs (standard API calls)
  • Note: This is the same as opening GitHub in your browser

docs.title: 4. 🐛 Bug Reports & Feedback (User-Initiated Only)
docs.text:
• What: Bug reports or feedback you explicitly choose to submit
  • When: Only when YOU click "Submit Bug Report" or "Send Feedback"
  • Data sent: Your description + optional system info
  • Data NOT sent: Any project data or conversations

docs.title: 5. 📊 Anonymized Usage Statistics
docs.text:
• What: Basic usage counters (e.g., "user opened dashboard 5 times")
  • When: Periodically, in the background
  • Data sent: Anonymized counts and feature usage
  • Data NOT sent: Any identifiable project data, code, or conversations

docs.title: API Key Security
docs.text:
• All API keys are stored locally in ~/.agentos/config.yaml
  • Keys are transmitted only to their respective services (GitHub token → GitHub API only)
  • The AgentOS web UI masks API keys in the settings panel
  • No API keys are ever logged or transmitted to UnicoLab

docs.title: Open Source Transparency
docs.text:
AgentOS is built on standard, auditable technologies:

  • Go — Compiled binary, no hidden dependencies
  • SQLite — Industry-standard local database
  • React — Standard web UI framework
  • Standard APIs — GitHub REST API, Jira REST API

docs.title: Questions?
docs.text:
If you have any security concerns or questions about our data handling, please:

  • Open an issue on GitHub
  • Contact us at security@unicolab.ai

docs.title: AI Copilot
docs.text: Your personal AI assistant that understands your projects, remembers your preferences, and helps you make better decisions.

docs.title: Streaming Chat
docs.text:
Talk to your agent in natural language. Ask questions about your projects, request reports, or get AI analysis of your team's activity.

The chat interface supports:

  • Real-time streaming — See responses as they're generated
  • Markdown rendering — Rich formatting with tables, code blocks, headers
  • Tool call visualization — Watch the Action Chain as your agent works
  • Context-aware — Your agent knows which project you're looking at

docs.title: Agent Memory
docs.text:
AgentOS includes JP Memory — an auto-learning system that:

  • Extracts facts from every conversation (team names, preferences, project details)
  • Remembers corrections — Tell it once, it remembers forever
  • Injects context — Relevant memories are automatically included in every reply
  • Fully local — All memories stored in your SQLite database

Press ++cmd+m++ to view and manage your agent's memory.

docs.title: Action Chain
docs.text:
When your agent uses tools (GitHub API, Jira, file system), you see the full Action Chain in real-time:

  • Each tool call shown with its name, arguments, and output
  • Per-step timing for performance visibility
  • Expandable detail panels
  • Full transparency — see exactly what your agent is doing

docs.title: Command Center
docs.text:
Two-tab interface for maximum productivity:

  1. Quick Commands — One-click PM prompts: standup, sprint status, risk analysis, blockers
  2. Pipeline Executions — Automated multi-step tool workflows

Press ++cmd+k++ for the Command Palette — spotlight-style search across all commands.

docs.title: Supported AI Providers
docs.text:
Provider Local? Cost Setup Ollama ✅ Yes Free Guide → OpenAI (GPT-4o) ❌ Cloud Paid API key Anthropic (Claude) ❌ Cloud Paid API key Google Gemini ❌ Cloud Free tier API key

Tip

For maximum privacy, use Ollama. Everything runs on your machine — no data ever leaves.

docs.title: Dashboard
docs.text: A 24-card, drag-and-drop bento-grid dashboard that gives you a complete picture of your project at a glance.

docs.title: Dashboard Cards
docs.text:
Every card is draggable, resizable, and interactive. Click any card's Ask JP button to get AI-powered analysis.

Card What it shows 📊 KPIs Open PRs, issues, commits, stars — dynamic labels based on fetch range 🎯 Risk Radar 0–100 risk score from stale PRs, low velocity, sprint lag 📈 Velocity Chart 8-week stacked author velocity chart 📅 Gantt Chart AI-generated timeline from real PR + sprint data 🎯 Action Center Centralized risks, AI recommendations, checkable task list 🏆 Team Leaderboard Gamified contributor ranking with streaks and badges 🌡️ Contribution Heatmap 26-week commit activity grid 🔀 Pull Requests Live PR list with AI reviewer suggestions 🐛 Issues Labeled issue tracker 📰 Daily Brief Auto-generated morning project summary 🧩 Sprint Status Jira sprint ring with WIP/Done/Todo 🗺️ Mind Map Jira issue visual map by type/label 🏗️ Epic Tracker Issues grouped by Epic with progress 📝 Notes Auto-saving markdown scratchpad per project 🔍 PR Dependencies Force graph of PR relationships 📅 Sprint Planner AI-generated sprint plans 📋 Scheduled Reports Automated report delivery 📄 Executive Report Boardroom-ready PDF export 🕐 Activity Timeline Full activity feed with weekly AI summaries 🔄 Update Log Data freshness and connector status

docs.title: Living View
docs.text:
Switch between Classic (bento grid) and Living (orbital) dashboard modes:

  • Classic — Traditional card grid, drag-and-drop customization
  • Living — Animated orbital view with JP bubble at center, interactive nodes for each widget

docs.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
docs.text: Shortcut Action ++cmd+r++ Refresh dashboard data ++cmd+k++ Command Palette ++cmd+comma++ Settings

docs.title: Fleet View
docs.text: Monitor all your projects at once — a multi-project health overview ranked by risk score.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Fleet View gives managers a bird's-eye view of all tracked projects. Each project shows:

  • Health Score — AI-calculated from PR activity, sprint progress, and team velocity
  • Risk Level — Color-coded: 🟢 Low · 🟡 Medium · 🔴 High
  • Key Metrics — Open PRs, active sprint items, recent commits
  • Quick Actions — Jump to dashboard, ask JP, or trigger a report

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Engineering Managers — Monitor 5–20 repos across teams at a glance
  • CTOs — Executive overview of all active projects
  • Tech Leads — Identify which projects need attention this week
  • Freelancers — Manage multiple client projects from one hub

docs.title: Accessing Fleet View
docs.text: Click Fleet in the sidebar, or press ++cmd+k++ and type "fleet".

docs.title: Integrations
docs.text: AgentOS connects to your existing development tools to aggregate project intelligence.

docs.title: GitHub
docs.text:
Multi-repository support with deep analytics:

  • Pull Requests — Status, reviewers, AI-powered reviewer suggestions
  • Commits — Activity charts, per-author breakdown, trend analysis
  • Issues — Tracking with labels and priority
  • Contributors — Activity metrics, velocity, leaderboard
  • Webhooks — Real-time push events

Setup Guide →

docs.title: Jira
docs.text:
Multi-board aggregation for sprint and project tracking:

  • Sprint Status — WIP, Done, Todo ring charts
  • Issues — By type, priority, label, and epic
  • Epic Tracker — Progress bars per epic
  • Mind Map — Visual issue hierarchy
  • Sprint Planner — AI-generated sprint plans

Setup Guide →

docs.title: Slack
docs.text:
Send reports and standup summaries to your team channels:

  • Standup Reports — One-click send to Slack
  • Notifications — Alert channels about risks and blockers

Setup Guide →

docs.title: AIFlow Platform
docs.text:
Sync project intelligence with the centralized AIFlow platform:

  • Push Reports — Share insights with your team
  • Multi-agent Orchestration — Connect to AIFlow's agentic workflows
  • Centralized Dashboard — View all projects across the organization

Setup Guide →

docs.title: AI Providers
docs.text:
Provider Type Models Privacy Ollama Local Llama 3.2, Mistral, CodeLlama ✅ 100% local OpenAI Cloud GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini API calls to OpenAI Anthropic Cloud Claude 3.5 Sonnet API calls to Anthropic Google Cloud Gemini 2.0 Flash API calls to Google

Ollama Setup Guide →

docs.title: Flavors
docs.text: AgentOS is built on a flavor system — each flavor is a specialized AI agent persona with its own personality, tools, dashboard configuration, and use case.

docs.title: What is a Flavor?
docs.text:
A flavor defines:

  • 🤖 Agent Persona — Name, personality, communication style, system prompt
  • 🧰 Available Tools — Which integrations and capabilities are enabled
  • 📊 Dashboard Layout — Which cards appear by default
  • ⚡ Quick Actions — One-click prompts tailored to the use case
  • 🎨 Branding — Icon, accent color, welcome message

docs.title: Available Flavors
docs.text:
🎩 Jean-Pierre — The PM

AI Project Management copilot. GitHub + Jira + Slack intelligence.

Status: ✅ Available Learn more → 🏢 Office Assistant

Document management, scheduling, and workflow automation.

Status: 🔜 Coming Soon 🛒 Retail Ops

Inventory, orders, and retail analytics assistant.

Status: 🔜 Coming Soon

docs.title: Custom Flavors
docs.text: AgentOS's flavor system is extensible -> to any use-case, tools, connectors etc ... Imagine all possibilities, we can create a flavor for any need, any industry, any use-case, any role !

docs.title: Jean-Pierre — The PM 🎩
docs.text:
"I don't just show you data — I understand your projects."

Jean-Pierre is the Project Management flavor of AgentOS. He's your AI copilot for tracking projects, synthesizing development activity, generating reports, and managing sprints.

Meet Jean-Pierre

Your AI Project Management Copilot

docs.title: 📊 Project Intelligence
docs.text:
• Connects to your GitHub repos and Jira boards
  • Aggregates data from multiple sources into one unified view
  • Calculates health scores, risk assessments, and velocity trends

docs.title: 📋 Report Generation
docs.text:
• Standup reports — One-click daily status updates
  • Sprint status — Progress, blockers, and capacity analysis
  • Executive summaries — Boardroom-ready project overviews
  • Risk analysis — AI-identified blockers and stale work items

docs.title: 🧠 Smart Memory
docs.text:
• Remembers your team structure, project priorities, and preferences
  • Learns from every conversation to give better answers over time
  • Extracts action items and decisions automatically

docs.title: 📅 Meeting Management
docs.text:
• Save, search, and export meeting notes
  • Track action items across meetings
  • AI-generated follow-up summaries

docs.title: Quick Actions
docs.text:
Jean-Pierre comes with pre-configured one-click prompts:

Action What it does 📊 List Projects Show all tracked projects with repos and Jira keys 📋 Standup Report Generate a structured standup with metrics and risks 🔍 Sprint Status Current sprint progress across all projects 📈 Project Progress Commit activity, sprint metrics, milestones, risks 🏗️ Sprint Burndown Task completion timeline and analysis 📤 Sync to AIFlow Push comprehensive report to AIFlow platform 📝 New Meeting Note Create structured meeting minutes with action items 📋 Meeting Actions Show all open action items from meetings 📖 Recent Meetings List and summarize recent meeting notes

docs.title: Structured Reports
docs.text:
Jean-Pierre generates rich, structured reports that render as interactive UI components:

  • :::report — Standup reports with metrics, risks, and actions
  • :::milestones — Release timelines and key dates
  • :::gantt — Sprint task breakdowns and burndown views

docs.title: Target Audience
docs.text:
• Engineering Managers — Track team velocity, PR throughput, sprint health
  • Technical PMs — Generate reports, identify blockers, coordinate cross-team
  • Freelance Developers — Manage multiple client projects from one hub
  • Startup CTOs — Real-time project intelligence without enterprise pricing

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: AgentOS stores all settings locally on your machine. You can configure everything through the web UI (Settings panel, ++cmd+comma++) or by editing config files directly.

docs.title: Config Files
docs.text: File Location Purpose config.yaml ~/.agentos/config.yaml AI provider, API keys, integrations projects.yaml ~/.agentos/projects.yaml Your tracked projects .watchtower.json Next to the binary License credentials

docs.title: AI Provider Settings
docs.text:
Choose one AI provider (or switch between them anytime):

Ollama (Free, Local)OpenAIAnthropicGoogle Gemini 
ollama_model: llama3.2:latest
ollama_url: http://localhost:11434   # default

See the Ollama Setup Guide for installation instructions.

openai_api_key: sk-proj-...
openai_model: gpt-4o-mini          # or gpt-4o, gpt-4-turbo

anthropic_api_key: sk-ant-...
anthropic_model: claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022

gemini_api_key: AIza...
gemini_model: gemini-2.0-flash

Recommendation

For privacy-first usage, we recommend Ollama — it runs AI models entirely on your machine with zero cloud dependencies. For best quality, GPT-4o or Claude 3.5 Sonnet are excellent choices.

docs.title: Projects Configuration
docs.text:
Projects are defined in ~/.agentos/projects.yaml:

projects:
  - name: My Product
    id: my-product
    color: "#6C63FF"
    github:
      repos:
        - myorg/frontend
        - myorg/backend         # multi-repo: all aggregated
        - myorg/infrastructure
      days_to_fetch: 30         # how far back to look
    jira:
      - project_key: PROD
        board_id: 42
        days_to_fetch: 14
    docs:                       # optional documentation links
      - label: Wiki
        url: https://wiki.example.com/my-product
    tags:
      - web
      - production

You can also manage projects through the Projects page in the web UI — no YAML editing needed.

docs.title: GitHub Configuration
docs.text:
github_token: ghp_...           # Personal Access Token (classic)

See GitHub Setup Guide for token creation instructions.

docs.title: Jira Configuration
docs.text:
jira_token: "your-api-token"
jira_email: you@company.com
jira_site_url: https://yourcompany.atlassian.net

See Jira Setup Guide for details.

docs.title: Slack Integration
docs.text:
slack_webhook_url: https://hooks.slack.com/services/T.../B.../...

See Slack Setup Guide for webhook creation.

docs.title: AIFlow Sync (Optional)
docs.text:
aiflow_url: https://your-aiflow-instance.com
aiflow_token: your-api-token
aiflow_project_id: "project-uuid"   # optional — links to a specific AIFlow project

See AIFlow Sync Guide for integration details.

docs.title: License Configuration
docs.text:
License credentials can be provided in three ways (in priority order):

1. License file (recommended):

Place .watchtower.json next to the agentos binary:

{
  "url": "https://watchtower.unicolab.io",
  "license_key": "your-license-key",
  "kid": "your-credential-kid",
  "secret": "your-credential-secret"
}

2. Environment variables:

export WATCHTOWER_LICENSE_KEY="your-license-key"
export WATCHTOWER_KID="your-credential-kid"
export WATCHTOWER_SECRET="your-credential-secret"

3. Config file (~/.agentos/config.yaml):

watchtower_url: https://watchtower.unicolab.io
watchtower_license_key: "your-license-key"
watchtower_kid: "your-credential-kid"
watchtower_secret: "your-credential-secret"

docs.title: Server Settings
docs.text: Setting Default Description port 18080 HTTP server port host 0.0.0.0 Bind address auto_open true Auto-open browser on start

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
All config values can also be set via environment variables (uppercase, prefixed with AGENTOS_):

export AGENTOS_OLLAMA_MODEL=llama3.2:latest
export AGENTOS_GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...
export AGENTOS_PORT=8080

Environment variables take precedence over config file values.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Getting AgentOS running on your machine takes less than 5 minutes. Pick your platform and follow the steps below.

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
Head to the Releases Page and download the archive matching your system:

Platform Architecture File macOS (Apple Silicon — M1/M2/M3/M4) arm64 agentos_*_darwin_arm64.tar.gz macOS (Intel) amd64 agentos_*_darwin_amd64.tar.gz Linux x86_64 agentos_*_linux_amd64.tar.gz Linux (ARM — Raspberry Pi, etc.) arm64 agentos_*_linux_arm64.tar.gz Windows x86_64 agentos_*_windows_amd64.zip

Not sure which macOS version?

Open Terminal and run: uname -m — If it says arm64, you have Apple Silicon — If it says x86_64, you have Intel

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
macOSLinuxWindows 
# 1. Extract the archive
tar xzf agentos_*_darwin_*.tar.gz

# 2. Move the binary to your PATH (so you can run it from anywhere)
sudo mv agentos /usr/local/bin/

# 3. Remove the quarantine flag (macOS security)
sudo xattr -rd com.apple.quarantine /usr/local/bin/agentos

# 4. Verify it works
agentos version

macOS Gatekeeper

Since AgentOS is not (yet) notarized with Apple, macOS may show a security warning the first time you run it. The xattr command above removes this. Alternatively, go to System Settings → Privacy & Security and click "Allow Anyway".

# 1. Extract the archive
tar xzf agentos_*_linux_*.tar.gz

# 2. Move the binary to your PATH
sudo mv agentos /usr/local/bin/

# 3. Make it executable (usually already set)
sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agentos

# 4. Verify it works
agentos version
  1. Extract the .zip archive to a folder (e.g., C:\Program Files\AgentOS\)
  2. Add to PATH (optional, for command-line access):
    • Open Settings → System → About → Advanced system settings
    • Click Environment Variables
    • Edit the Path variable and add C:\Program Files\AgentOS\
  3. Double-click agentos.exe to launch, or run from PowerShell:
cd "C:\Program Files\AgentOS"
.\agentos.exe serve

docs.title: First Run — Setup Wizard
docs.text:
When you launch AgentOS for the first time, it will guide you through an interactive setup wizard:

agentos setup

The wizard will help you configure:

  1. AI Provider — Choose between Ollama (free, local), OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google Gemini
  2. GitHub Connection — Connect your GitHub repos for project tracking
  3. Jira Connection — (Optional) Connect Jira boards for sprint data
  4. License Key — Enter your Watchtower license key

No license key yet?

We offer free beta licenses for early adopters! See the License page for details on how to get one.

docs.title: Quick Launch
docs.text:
Once setup is done, start AgentOS:

# Start the server
agentos serve

# Your browser will open automatically at:
# → http://localhost:18080

That's it! 🎉 AgentOS is now running on your machine with a premium web interface.

docs.title: Run as a Background Service
docs.text:
Want AgentOS to start automatically when you log in?

macOS (launchd)Linux (systemd) 
# Install as a user service
agentos serve --install-service

# Check status
launchctl list | grep agentos

# Uninstall later
agentos serve --uninstall-service

# Install as a user service
agentos serve --install-service

# Check status
systemctl --user status agentos

# Uninstall later
agentos serve --uninstall-service

Once installed as a service, AgentOS runs in the background and is always available at http://localhost:18080.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Quick Start Guide → — 5-minute walkthrough of your first session
  • Set up Ollama for free local AI → — Run AI completely on your machine
  • Connect GitHub → — Track your repositories

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Follow this 5-minute guide to go from zero to your first AI-powered project insight.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, make sure you have:

  • [x] AgentOS installed — See the Installation Guide
  • [x] An AI provider — Either Ollama (free, local) or an API key (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini)
  • [x] A GitHub repo (optional) — For project tracking features

docs.title: Step 1: Run the Setup Wizard
docs.text:
If you haven't already run setup:

agentos setup

The wizard will walk you through each step interactively. You can also configure everything later through the web UI settings panel.

docs.title: Step 2: Start AgentOS
docs.text:
agentos serve

Your browser will automatically open to http://localhost:18080. You'll see the Welcome Screen with your agent ready to go.

docs.title: Step 3: Create Your First Project
docs.text:
1. Click Projects in the sidebar
  2. Click + New Project
  3. Fill in:
    • Project name — e.g., "My Web App"
    • GitHub repos — e.g., myorg/my-web-app
    • Color — Pick a color for visual identification
  4. Click Create

AgentOS will immediately start fetching data from your GitHub repos.

docs.title: Step 4: Talk to Your Agent
docs.text:
Open the chat panel (right sidebar) and try asking:

Try these prompts

  • "Give me a standup report for My Web App"
  • "What are the open pull requests?"
  • "Show me the sprint status"
  • "Who has been most active this week?"
  • "What are the biggest risks in this project?"

Your agent will use tools to fetch real-time data from your connected sources and generate structured, actionable reports.

docs.title: Step 5: Explore the Dashboard
docs.text:
Switch to the Dashboard view to see your project at a glance:

  • KPIs — Open PRs, issues, commits, contributors
  • Risk Radar — AI-scored risk assessment
  • Velocity Chart — Commit activity over time
  • Gantt Chart — AI-generated timeline from real data
  • Action Center — Recommended next steps

Every card is draggable and customizable. Click any card's Ask JP button to get AI analysis of that specific metric.

docs.title: Step 6: Use Quick Commands
docs.text:
Press ++cmd+k++ (macOS) or ++ctrl+k++ (Linux/Windows) to open the Command Palette — a spotlight-style search that lets you:

  • Navigate to any view
  • Run common PM workflows
  • Ask your agent a quick question
  • Trigger tool executions

docs.title: What's Next?
docs.text:
🤖 Set up Ollama

Run AI 100% locally for free

Guide → 🐙 Connect GitHub

Track repos, PRs, and contributor activity

Guide → 📋 Connect Jira

Track sprints, issues, and epics

Deep-dive into all settings

docs.title: AIFlow Sync
docs.text: Connect AgentOS to the AIFlow platform for centralized project intelligence and team-wide AI workflows.

docs.title: What is AIFlow Sync?
docs.text:
AIFlow Sync lets you push reports, summaries, and project intelligence from your local AgentOS instance to a centralized AIFlow platform. This is useful for:

  • Sharing insights with your team
  • Centralized project dashboards
  • Multi-agent orchestration

Optional Feature

AIFlow Sync is entirely optional. AgentOS works perfectly as a standalone, local-first tool without it.

docs.title: Configure
docs.text:
aiflow_url: https://your-aiflow-instance.com
aiflow_token: your-api-token
aiflow_project_id: "project-uuid"   # optional

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
• Ask: "Sync this report to AIFlow"
  • Use the AIFlow Sync button in the header
  • AI will push structured reports via aiflow_sync.push_report

docs.title: Background Service
docs.text: Run AgentOS as a persistent background service that starts automatically when you log in.

docs.title: Install as a Service
docs.text:
macOS (launchd)Linux (systemd)Windows 
agentos serve --install-service

This creates a launchd user agent that: - Starts automatically on login - Keeps AgentOS running in the background - Restarts automatically if it crashes - Accessible at http://localhost:18080

Manage the service: 

# Check status
launchctl list | grep agentos

# Stop
launchctl bootout gui/$(id -u) ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.unicolab.agentos.plist

# Start
launchctl bootstrap gui/$(id -u) ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.unicolab.agentos.plist

# Uninstall
agentos serve --uninstall-service


agentos serve --install-service

Manage the service: 

# Check status
systemctl --user status agentos

# Stop
systemctl --user stop agentos

# Start
systemctl --user start agentos

# View logs
journalctl --user -u agentos -f

# Uninstall
agentos serve --uninstall-service

On Windows, simply add agentos.exe serve to your Startup folder:

  1. Press ++win+r++, type shell:startup, press Enter
  2. Create a shortcut to agentos.exe serve in this folder

Alternatively, run as a service using NSSM.

docs.title: Verify
docs.text:
After installing, close and reopen your terminal (or log out and back in), then:

# Check if it's running
curl http://localhost:18080/v1/health
# → {"status":"ok"}

Open your browser to http://localhost:18080 — AgentOS is always there.

docs.title: GitHub Setup
docs.text: Connect AgentOS to your GitHub repositories to track PRs, commits, issues, and contributor activity in real-time.

docs.title: Create a Personal Access Token
docs.text:
1. Go to github.com/settings/tokens
  2. Click Generate new token (classic)
  3. Give it a descriptive name (e.g., "AgentOS")
  4. Select these scopes:
    • [x] repo — Full control of private repositories
    • [x] read:org — Read org membership (optional, for org repos)
  5. Click Generate token
  6. Copy the token — you won't see it again!

Keep your token safe

Your token is stored locally in ~/.agentos/config.yaml and is never sent anywhere except directly to GitHub's API from your machine.

docs.title: Option 1: Settings UI (easiest)
docs.text:
1. Open AgentOS at http://localhost:18080
  2. Press ++cmd+comma++ to open Settings
  3. Navigate to Integrations → GitHub
  4. Paste your token
  5. Click Save

docs.title: Option 2: Config File
docs.text:
Add to ~/.agentos/config.yaml:

github_token: ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

docs.title: Add GitHub Repos to a Project
docs.text:
Once your token is configured, add repos to a project:

  1. Go to Projects → Select or create a project
  2. Under GitHub, add your repository paths:
    • Format: owner/repo-name
    • Example: facebook/react, myorg/my-app
  3. You can add multiple repos — they'll be aggregated in the dashboard
# Example in projects.yaml
projects:
  - name: My Product
    github:
      repos:
        - myorg/frontend
        - myorg/backend
        - myorg/docs
      days_to_fetch: 30

docs.title: What You'll See
docs.text:
Once connected, your dashboard will show:

  • KPIs — Open PRs, issues, commit count, contributors
  • PR List — All open PRs with review status and AI reviewer suggestions
  • Commit Chart — Activity over time with per-author breakdown
  • Velocity Chart — 8-week contributor velocity
  • Contribution Heatmap — 26-week activity grid
  • Risk Radar — AI-scored risk from stale PRs, missing reviewers, etc.

docs.title: Webhooks (Optional, Advanced)
docs.text:
For real-time updates (instead of polling), you can set up a GitHub webhook:

  1. In your GitHub repo, go to Settings → Webhooks
  2. Add webhook:
    • URL: http://your-machine:18080/v1/webhook/github
    • Content type: application/json
    • Secret: (optional, for HMAC verification)
    • Events: Push, Pull requests, Issues

Note

Webhooks require your machine to be reachable from the internet. For local development, this is typically not needed — AgentOS polls GitHub automatically.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Set up Jira →
  • Set up Slack →
  • Back to Quick Start →

docs.title: Jira Setup
docs.text: Connect AgentOS to your Jira boards to track sprints, issues, epics, and team workload.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• A Jira Cloud account (Atlassian)
  • An API token from Atlassian

docs.title: Create an API Token
docs.text:
1. Go to id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens
  2. Click Create API token
  3. Give it a label (e.g., "AgentOS")
  4. Copy the token

docs.title: Option 1: Settings UI
docs.text:
1. Open Settings (++cmd+comma++)
  2. Navigate to Integrations → Jira
  3. Fill in:
    • Site URL: https://yourcompany.atlassian.net
    • Email: Your Atlassian account email
    • API Token: The token you just created
  4. Click Save

docs.title: Option 2: Config File
docs.text:
jira_site_url: https://yourcompany.atlassian.net
jira_email: you@company.com
jira_token: "your-api-token"

docs.title: Add Jira Boards to a Project
docs.text:
projects:
  - name: My Product
    jira:
      - project_key: PROD
        board_id: 42
        days_to_fetch: 14
      - project_key: INFRA      # multiple boards supported
        board_id: 55

Finding your Board ID

Open your Jira board in a browser. The URL will look like: https://yourcompany.atlassian.net/jira/software/projects/PROD/boards/42 The number at the end (42) is your board ID.

docs.title: What You'll See
docs.text:
• Sprint Status — Ring chart showing Done/WIP/Todo
  • Epic Tracker — Issues grouped by Epic with progress bars
  • Mind Map — Visual map of issues by type and label
  • Sprint Planner — AI-generated sprint plans from backlog data

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Set up Slack →
  • Back to Quick Start →

docs.title: Ollama Setup — Free Local AI
docs.text: Ollama lets you run powerful AI models entirely on your machine — no API keys, no costs, no data leaving your computer. This is the recommended setup for maximum privacy.

docs.title: Install Ollama
docs.text:
macOSLinuxWindows 
# Install via Homebrew (recommended)
brew install ollama

# Or download from https://ollama.com/download

# One-line installer
curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

Download the installer from ollama.com/download and run it.

docs.title: Download a Model
docs.text:
After installing Ollama, pull a model. We recommend starting with Llama 3.2 — it's fast, capable, and free:

# Pull the default model (recommended for most systems)
ollama pull llama3.2

# For more powerful systems (16GB+ RAM), try the larger version
ollama pull llama3.2:70b

# For lightweight systems (8GB RAM), use the smaller version
ollama pull llama3.2:1b

docs.title: Model Recommendations
docs.text: Model RAM Needed Quality Speed Best For llama3.2:1b 4GB ⭐⭐ ⚡⚡⚡ Low-resource machines llama3.2 (8b) 8GB ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ⚡⚡ Most users (recommended) llama3.2:70b 48GB ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ ⚡ Best quality, needs powerful hardware mistral 8GB ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ⚡⚡ Good alternative codellama 8GB ⭐⭐⭐ ⚡⚡ Code-focused tasks

docs.title: Start Ollama
docs.text:
Ollama runs as a background service:

# Start the Ollama server (runs on port 11434)
ollama serve

# Verify it's running
curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags

Auto-start

On macOS, Ollama auto-starts when you open the app. On Linux, it runs as a systemd service automatically after installation.

docs.title: Option 1: Setup Wizard
docs.text: Run agentos setup and select Ollama as your AI provider. It will auto-detect available models.

docs.title: Option 2: Settings UI
docs.text:
1. Open AgentOS at http://localhost:18080
  2. Press ++cmd+comma++ to open Settings
  3. Under AI Provider, select Ollama
  4. Choose your model from the dropdown

docs.title: Option 3: Config File
docs.text:
Edit ~/.agentos/config.yaml:

ollama_model: llama3.2:latest
ollama_url: http://localhost:11434

docs.title: Verify It Works
docs.text:
1. Start AgentOS: agentos serve
  2. Open the chat panel
  3. Ask: "Hello, introduce yourself"

If everything is configured correctly, you'll see the agent respond using your local Ollama model — completely offline, completely private.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
AgentOS can't connect to Ollama

Make sure Ollama is running: ollama serve Check the URL in your config matches (default: http://localhost:11434)

Model is too slow

Try a smaller model: ollama pull llama3.2:1b Close other memory-heavy applications

Out of memory errors

Your chosen model needs more RAM than available. Switch to a smaller model or close other applications.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Connect GitHub →
  • Connect Jira →
  • Back to Quick Start →

docs.title: Slack Setup
docs.text: Send standup reports and notifications directly to your Slack channels.

docs.title: Create a Webhook
docs.text:
1. Go to api.slack.com/apps
  2. Click Create New App → From scratch
  3. Name it "AgentOS" and select your workspace
  4. Go to Incoming Webhooks → Toggle On
  5. Click Add New Webhook to Workspace
  6. Select the channel (e.g., #standups)
  7. Copy the Webhook URL

docs.title: Configure
docs.text:
Add to Settings or ~/.agentos/config.yaml:

slack_webhook_url: https://hooks.slack.com/services/T.../B.../...

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Ask your agent: "Send a standup report to Slack for My Project"

Or use the Send to Slack button in the Standup Composer.

docs.title: API Endpoints
docs.text: AgentOS exposes a REST API for programmatic access. All endpoints are available at http://localhost:18080 when the server is running.

docs.title: Chat
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description POST /v1/chat/stream Streaming SSE chat with the agent

docs.title: Projects
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /v1/projects List all projects GET /v1/projects/{id} Get project details GET /v1/projects/{id}/insights Full dashboard data (KPIs, charts, etc.) GET /v1/projects/{id}/prs/{num}/suggest-reviewer AI-powered reviewer suggestion GET /v1/projects/{id}/briefs Daily briefings POST /v1/projects/{id}/standup/send Send standup to Slack

docs.title: Fleet
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /v1/fleet/summary Multi-project health overview

docs.title: Memory
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description PUT /v1/memory/{ns}/{key} Store a memory GET /v1/memory/{ns} Search memories by namespace POST /v1/memory/extract AI-extract memories from conversation

docs.title: Usage & Monitoring
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /v1/usage LLM token usage + cost tracking DELETE /v1/usage Clear usage data GET /v1/health Health check

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description POST /v1/webhook/github GitHub webhook receiver

docs.title: CLI Commands
docs.text: AgentOS provides a powerful command-line interface for all operations.

docs.title: Main Commands
docs.text:
agentos serve          # Start HTTP server + web UI (default)
agentos chat           # Interactive CLI chat session
agentos run "message"  # Execute a single message and exit
agentos setup          # Interactive first-time setup wizard
agentos config         # View/edit configuration
agentos projects       # Manage tracked projects
agentos license        # Check Watchtower license status
agentos version        # Print version, commit, and build date

docs.title: agentos serve
docs.text:
Start the HTTP API server with embedded web UI.

agentos serve                      # Default: http://localhost:18080
agentos serve --port 8080          # Custom port
agentos serve --no-open            # Don't auto-open browser
agentos serve --install-service    # Install as background service
agentos serve --uninstall-service  # Remove background service

docs.title: agentos chat
docs.text: Start an interactive CLI chat session with your agent.

docs.title: Slash Commands
docs.text: Command Description /help Show all commands /tools List available tools /models List available models /model <name> Switch model /sessions List active sessions /session <id> Switch session /new Start a new session /memory Show agent memory /forget Clear agent memory /history Show recent runs /events <id> Show events for a run /license Show license status /feedback Submit feedback /bugreport Report a bug /clear Clear screen /quit Exit

docs.title: agentos setup
docs.text:
Interactive wizard for first-time configuration. Walks you through:

  1. AI provider selection and configuration
  2. GitHub token setup
  3. Jira credentials (optional)
  4. License key entry

docs.title: agentos version
docs.text:
$ agentos version
Jean-Pierre v0.8.1 (abc1234) built 2026-03-10T12:00:00Z
Pack: aiflow-pm (Jean-Pierre — The PM 🎩)
License: 🟢 enterprise (active) [3/10 seats]

docs.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
docs.text: Master these shortcuts to navigate AgentOS like a pro.

docs.title: Global Shortcuts
docs.text: Shortcut Action ++cmd+k++ Open Command Palette ++cmd+m++ Open JP Memory panel ++cmd+n++ New chat session ++cmd+t++ Tool Explorer ++cmd+comma++ Provider Settings ++cmd+r++ Refresh dashboard data ++slash++ Focus chat input ++question++ Show keyboard shortcuts reference ++escape++ Close any open panel
